What's up everyone? So if you have a cancellation, so like we just did the signup process, the cancellation checklist is real easy. You get a message regarding cancellation or something like that and all you got to do is request a time to talk, okay. You'll be like, hey, like we're not gonna try and sell you anything, like this is just, and if you want you can add that to your front-end contract just saying we do require you that you just talk with success coach if you do choose to leave. And most times you can even sell that, like we really want to figure out if you're gonna like if you're just feeling unmotivated or whatever it is. So don't be aggressive on these phone calls because these are like right when people are literally about to leave so they're likely to if they're if they're pissed, you know, they're not happy about stuff like they could leave negative reviews. So just don't be aggressive, basically just try and save your reputation and just say, hey, like I just want to figure out what we could have done better. Like here's an example of some brutal honesty we've got bathrooms are disgusting, the trainers weren't paying any attention, they were on their cell phone the whole time. I stopped being motivated, the workouts are really boring or they're repetitive or the music sucks, like give me an idea so that I can work on it and I can take it back to my drummer so I can prove my business so that like I continue to feed my family. You know what I mean? If you can phrase it like that, most people are gonna be willing to say, okay, I'll help this guy out. And after a lot of times after someone airs agreements, be like that is a whole like that sucks, Kathy and I'm really really sorry. Will you give me the opportunity to like when you're business back like this next month, like it's on me because of the experience that you said you had. And like let me make a huge effort and if you don't see a big difference in that after the month, don't worry about it. And then do your best to try and fix it. Now they're like, no, I'm done. I'm fine, I'm out or whatever. Then after that all you gotta do is just color them red on the master roster sheet, which is same thing. This puppy, go back to his year and you contact that you know you color them and then you say after their name, you know, whatever it is and then you color that thing red. All right, so real simple. And then change the number of the active clients in the top left by one. So this becomes way to do seven right. Really easy peasy there. And then remove them from automated billing, obviously in your billing processor and then add them to the skibbio group for canceled customers. And that way we're going to try and win them back so you can we'll push campaigns out to customers who've been there, which a lot of times they're actually really high quality customers because they've actually been into your gym. So sometimes like they're sometimes people do get busier. They you know, it's not actually your fault. So we should have those people like, oh yeah, I did like that gym. I'll give them another shot. And this is not hard, especially if you just text them and it seems like it's a personal text. So that is the cancellation process and we do this on the back end because we're going to we're going to try and win them back. If you can win back canceled people, then it actually increases your lifetime value of all your customers, which is another way to increase how much you can work it.
